meta.summary: ByteDance Seed launched Seed 2.0 alongside its Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM and the Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, signaling a serious push into real-time voice interaction and broader foundation-model deployment. Zhipu AI unveiled GLM-5 as its new flagship agent-engineering model, claiming open-source state-of-the-art coding and agent performance with results comparable to Claude Opus 4.5 on SWE-bench Verified and Terminal Bench 2.0. On the research side, "Judge Circuits" highlighted a core reliability issue in LLM-as-a-judge evaluation by showing score shifts caused purely by output-format changes, a meaningful warning for teams relying on automated benchmarking.

source_sections.label: Research Papers
source_sections.description: Research papers and paper-trending signals tied to the current day.

items.title: Judge Circuits
items.summary_short: LLM-as-a-judge has become the dominant paradigm for grading model outputs at scale, yet the same model assigns systematically different scores when its output format changes (e.g., a 1-5 rating vs. a True/False label).

topic_refs.headline: Judge Circuits Paper Exposes Format Instability in LLM-as-a-Judge Systems

items.title: BAPR: Bayesian amnesic piecewise-robust reinforcement learning for non-stationary continuous control
items.summary_short: Real-world control systems frequently operate under \emph{piecewise stationary} conditions, where dynamics remain stable for extended periods before undergoing abrupt regime changes.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

topic_refs.headline: BAPR: Bayesian amnesic piecewise-robust reinforcement learning for non-stationary continuous control

items.title: A Few GPUs, A Whole Lotta Scale: Faithful LLM Training Emulation with PrismLLM
items.summary_short: Large language model (LLM) training today runs on clusters spanning thousands of GPUs. While this scale enables rapid model advances, developing, debugging, and performance-tuning the training framework inevitably becomes complex and costly.

topic_refs.headline: A Few GPUs, A Whole Lotta Scale: Faithful LLM Training Emulation with PrismLLM

items.title: $\phi$-Balancing for Mixture-of-Experts Training
items.summary_short: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models rely on balanced expert utilization to fully realize their scalability. However, existing load-balancing methods are largely heuristic and operate on noisy mini-batch assignment statistics, introducing bias relative to population-level objectives.

topic_refs.headline: $\phi$-Balancing for Mixture-of-Experts Training

items.title: Syntax Without Semantics: Teaching Large Language Models to Code in an Unseen Language
items.summary_short: Large language models (LLMs) achieve high pass rates on code generation benchmarks, yet whether they can transfer this ability to languages absent from pretraining remains poorly understood.

topic_refs.headline: Syntax Without Semantics: Teaching Large Language Models to Code in an Unseen Language

items.title: Agentic Discovery of Neural Architectures: AIRA-Compose and AIRA-Design
items.summary_short: Toward recursive self-improvement, we investigate LLM agents autonomously designing foundation models beyond standard Transformers. We introduce a dual-framework approach: AIRA-Compose for high-level architecture search, and AIRA-Design for low-level mechanistic implementation.

topic_refs.headline: Agentic Discovery of Neural Architectures: AIRA-Compose and AIRA-Design

items.title: On the Stability of Growth in Structural Plasticity
items.summary_short: Standard deep-learning pipelines usually choose the network architecture before training and keep it fixed throughout optimization. In contrast, a model can also be adapted by editing its structure during training, for example by pruning existing hidden-neuron units or growing new ones.

items.title: Imperfect World Models are Exploitable
items.summary_short: We propose a novel definition of model exploitation in reinforcement learning. Informally, a world model is exploitable if it implies that one policy should be strictly preferred over another while the environment's true transition model implies the reverse.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

items.title: GQLA: Group-Query Latent Attention for Hardware-Adaptive Large Language Model Decoding
items.summary_short: Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA), the attention used in DeepSeek-V2/V3, jointly compresses keys and values into a low-rank latent and matches the H100 roofline almost perfectly.

items.title: GQA-{\mu}P: The maximal parameterization update for grouped query attention
items.summary_short: Hyperparameter transfer across model architectures dramatically reduces the amount of compute necessary for tuning large language models (LLMs).

items.title: Feedback World Model Enables Precise Guidance of Diffusion Policy
items.summary_short: World models aim to improve robotic decision making by predicting the consequences of actions. However, in practice, their predictions often become unreliable once the robot encounters states outside the training distribution, limiting their effectiveness at deployment.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

items.title: Characterizing Learning in Deep Neural Networks using Tractable Algorithmic Complexity Analysis
items.summary_short: Training large-scale deep neural networks (DNNs) is resource-intensive, making model compression a practical necessity. The widely accepted ''learning as compression'' hypothesis posits that training induces structure in network weights, which enables compression.

featured_topics.headline: Seed 2.0 Launch Puts ByteDance Seed Into Real-Time Multimodal Competition
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, a speech model focused on attentive listening, robust interference suppression, and more natural interaction. The announcement positions it as a new model launch in the company’s speech AI lineup
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance is expanding from foundation models into real-time speech interaction, a strategic signal for investors and operators tracking the race toward always-on AI assistants and voice-native agents.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed announced three linked artifacts at once: the core Seed 2.0 model, the Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, and the Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview release on Arena for early evaluation.

The Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM was positioned around attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural interaction, indicating a focus on low-latency, interruption-tolerant voice experiences rather than text-only chat.

Releasing Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena suggests ByteDance wants external comparison and developer visibility early, a common tactic when vendors are trying to establish benchmark credibility and ecosystem pull.

evidence.title: Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Model Release on Arena
Introducing Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM

featured_topics.headline: GLM-5 Becomes Zhipu AI’s Flagship Model for Agent Engineering
featured_topics.summary_short: Zhipu AI announced GLM-5 as a flagship base model aimed at agent engineering, highlighting open-source SOTA performance in coding and agent capabilities. The page also claims strong results on SWE-bench Verified and Terminal Bench 2.0, comparable to Claude Opus 4.5
featured_topics.why_it_matters: GLM-5 strengthens the China open-model ecosystem and gives enterprises another serious candidate for coding and agent workflows outside the U.S. frontier-model stack.
key_takeaways: Zhipu AI presented GLM-5 as its new flagship foundation model specifically optimized for agent engineering, not just general chatbot use.
The announcement claims open-source state-of-the-art performance in coding and agent tasks, with benchmark references including SWE-bench Verified and Terminal Bench 2.0.

Zhipu explicitly framed GLM-5 as comparable to Claude Opus 4.5 on those evaluations, signaling an attempt to compete directly with premium closed-model incumbents in software and autonomous task execution.

evidence.title: GLM-5 announced as Zhipu AI's new flagship foundation model for agent engineering

featured_topics.headline: Gemini Intelligence Rollout Extends Google’s AI Stack Across Android Devices
featured_topics.summary_short: This item says Android is entering a Gemini Intelligence era, indicating a new phase of Gemini integration across the Android platform. It points to a product update centered on embedding Google's AI features more deeply into Android experiences
featured_topics.why_it_matters: Google’s Gemini integration into Android signals that model advantage is increasingly being converted into distribution advantage through operating systems and consumer hardware.
key_takeaways: Coverage from The Rundown AI and Superhuman AI indicates Google is pushing Gemini-branded intelligence deeper into Android, broadening exposure beyond standalone chatbot products.
One cited angle described a 'Gemini-powered laptop,' implying Google’s AI positioning now spans both mobile and adjacent device categories rather than remaining confined to phones.

For enterprise buyers and investors, the key implication is that Google can bundle Gemini into default user experiences at platform scale, which may lower customer-acquisition costs relative to standalone AI apps.

evidence.title: 4 min read Googlebook: the Gemini-powered laptop is here
Android enters its Gemini Intelligence era

featured_topics.headline: Judge Circuits Paper Exposes Format Instability in LLM-as-a-Judge Systems
featured_topics.summary_short: LLM-as-a-judge has become the dominant paradigm for grading model outputs at scale, yet the same model assigns systematically different scores when its output format changes (e.g., a 1-5 rating vs. a True/False label).
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If automated evaluators change their scores based on output format rather than substance, product, research, and procurement decisions built on those evaluations can be materially distorted.
key_takeaways: "Judge Circuits" examines the increasingly important LLM-as-a-judge setup and finds that the same model can score identical content differently when the response schema changes, such as 1-5 ratings versus binary labels.
The paper pushes beyond surface-level observations of benchmark inconsistency by analyzing the mechanism behind these formatting effects, making it more actionable for model-evaluation teams.

The result matters for labs and enterprises using judge models in reinforcement learning, internal eval suites, and leaderboard reporting, where apparent gains may reflect prompt or format artifacts rather than genuine capability improvements.

evidence.title: Judge Circuits
